content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to buy 222 ice creams, each costing XXX dollars, for him and Chefina.

However, he only has YYY dollars with him. Will he be able to buy 222 ice creams?


Input Format

The only line of input will contain 222 space-separated integers XXX and YYY, the price of each ice cream and the money Chef has.


Output Format
Print `YES` if Chef will be able to buy two ice creams, otherwise print `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y — the price of one ice cream and the amount of money Chef has.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing "YES" if Chef can buy two ice creams (i.e., 2·X ≤ Y), otherwise print "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Total cost = 2*5 = 10 which is exactly the money Chef has, so he can buy the ice creams.
Total cost = 2*6 = 12 which exceeds Chef's 11 dollars, so he cannot buy them.
Case 1: 2*1=2 >1 → NO. Case 2: 2*10^9=2*10^9 ≤ 2*10^9 → YES. Case 3: 2*7=14 ≤13? No, actually 14>13, so output should be NO. Correction: 2*7=14 >13 → NO. Updated output: "NO\nYES\nNO".

solution.approach: For each test case read X and Y. Compute the total cost needed for two ice creams as 2*X. If this value is less than or equal to Y output YES, else output NO. The operation is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it)); Y = int(next(it))
        out.append('YES' if 2*X <= Y else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
